repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: prior spectral/equality-frontier family; API panel entries are Q1 FWL/spectral-threshold, not low-rank MC margins

published_prior_art.cite: SingerCucuringu2010 + Chatterjee2020: deterministic low-rank completion uniqueness/solvability, but not causal ATT margin constants

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: same internal MC/SDiD/ASCM estimator-duality angle, now refined to a staggered spectral recovery margin

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyBayatiDoudchenkoImbensKhosravi2021 + ArkhangelskyAtheyHirshbergImbensWager2021 + SingerCucuringu2010 + Chatterjee2020: no verified source gives finite staggered-mask iff plus MC/SDiD/ASCM first-order-law equality

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: open-set separation outside estimator-equality frontier; current staggered below-threshold version is adjacent

published_prior_art.cite: SingerCucuringu2010 + Chatterjee2020: nonuniqueness/failure of low-rank completion is known; causal ATT and estimator-law layer is extra

repo_prior_art.cite: panel_sdid_mc_id_v1: penalty-stable equality frontier for ASCM/SDiD/MC; current tuning-neutral margin interval is a refined variant

published_prior_art.cite: Heiniger2024 + BaiNg2021: model selection/factor imputation, not a common tuning-neutral MC/SDiD/ASCM first-order law

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The below-threshold witness sets b(D)=0 with no untreated period-4 anchor at all, so the separation hides entirely in unobserved counterfactuals; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship until a nondegenerate below-threshold witness with some target-period support is added.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey still misses Agarwal-Dahleh-Shah-Shen, 'Causal Matrix Completion' (arXiv 2021 / PMLR 2023), a close causal-matrix-completion comparator for identification and entrywise inference under nonrandom missingness.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed 'population SDiD weights' are not the published SDiD functional: Arkhangelsky et al. solve separate unit/time weighting problems and then a weighted DID regression, whereas the proposal writes an additive residual objective in omega_i and lambda_s; a_sdid is therefore not pinned to the named estimator.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: Ridge ASCM is defined only as weights plus an unspecified 'corresponding ridge outcome-regression augmentation', so the Frechet derivative a_ascm is not a fully defined population functional.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the SDiD block with the actual Arkhangelsky et al. population unit-weight objective, time-weight objective, and weighted DID estimand, then define the derivative of that map.
State the ridge ASCM augmentation algebra explicitly enough that a_ascm is a single-valued functional before comparing first-order laws.
Add a nondegenerate below-threshold staggered witness with b(D)>0 or at least some untreated support in every target period, showing ATT separation is not just absence of controls.
Add Agarwal-Dahleh-Shah-Shen as a comparator and distinguish SNN/causal MC identification from the proposed MC-SDiD-ASCM equality frontier.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: fix the SDiD/ASCM population functionals and replace the b(D)=0 below-threshold witness with a nondegenerate staggered witness before resubmitting for flagship review.
